reviews.review_msg: Tough homework assignments, would advise asking for help on these. Other than that attend all classes and you should get a great grade.
John's a nice enough guy, but he's not the best teacher. He posts videos in addition to his lectures, and you're better off just watching those at home and saving yourself the trip to campus.
Better profs available for 220!
He is great nowadays. He is underrated here.

First day of class stated "I mean if you use AI for your homework you could only get a B at best", so dont use it. 
Homework is worth 66% of the mark, and on the biweekly homeworks sometimes the students would get 0 feedback on their homework. Students complained in class and he simply said "I dont have time to give feedback for everyone"...

Was my first semester so no background knowledge. Tests were open book and quite fair. Assignments often went outside of material he had taught you so you had to teach yourself. Readings were smaller pieces of larger texts. Finding the larger texts online could often help for learning concepts relevant to assignments. Overall just a solid prof.

Lectures consistent of student presentations of an article every week and the rest of the class is him talking about a subject he wants to. Things were not well organized, and hard to study for. An 80 was considered a C and we did not know this at all. He is a very nice guy though and I liked him as a person. Though over all not a very hard class.

As other people mentioned here, the material was taught as if we already had some background knowledge on it, so I had to spend a lot of time outside class going over it. The grade distribution was not clear but I got B+ with ~80%. Tests were relatively easy compared to the homework and the amount of time I spent figuring the material out.
AVOID HIM TRUST

I've taken many ling courses and this = one of the most difficult due to lack of communication and clear instruction alone. He is very knowledgebale, 100%, but he assumes you know more than you do and doesn't offer much foundational help. Difficult to reach both prof and TAs out of class. Very, very frustrating tbh, feeling #abysmal sorry john

Not hard to get a good grade, but is very frustrating to learn under. He develops his own way of writing and doesn't explain what most of it means, leaving you to decipher his system on your own. Assumes that you already know far too much, especially for an intro Ling course.

He's a good guy, unfortunately not in COGS himself so he can't speak to a lot of the CS or Psyc topics, engaging and teaches well though. Vague in marking as everyone else says but you can also just ask him questions over email or before/after class and he will answer. He's reasonable and if you have issues with group mates you can talk to him.

he deserves his rating. does not have clear criteria at all. gives you a grade without specific explanation. classes boring. does not even use canvas to remind things and assign grades. makes you frustrated about morphology. please definitely take 323 with another prof

Alderete's teaching philosophy is great if you are interested in the course material and willing to participate. Quizzes and homework are fair and answers are covered in detail in the following class.

He is a knowledgeable professor, even though sometimes it took time for me to understand the content, he was willing to help and reply emails fast. Read textbook really helped me. Teaching style did not suit me, but he is a good professor, just ask him question if you are confused. Quizzes are NOT easy.

People told me John was bad news. As a lecturer, he isnt good at explaining things. When you ask a question he either refers you to the syllabus or asks you a question in return. Very lecture heavy, but I took the course as SS in 5 weeks, instead of 13. As the course was REMOTE, he wasnt really teaching, but talking AT us - goes too fast, keep up!

John Alderete is a good professor and he knows what he's talking about, although he lacks bedside manner and most often won't answer your question if it's content-related. I took this class summer session, so it was condensed into five weeks instead of thirteen, which worked well. The homework was hard but he'll help you if you ask for it.

Horrible prof, his hand-writing is awful and his explanation is confusing , I suggest you avoid this guy if you are Asian , also, if you visit his office hour , he wont be nice to you and try to kick you out ASAP , his grading is tough and most of the time you dont know why you get wrong because there are no explanation. Class averages are C

John is a very difficult dude. Considering taking him for a course? Sit the semester out and wait for a different professor to teach it. Seriously. You won't get much support from this guy, and you'll learn much more from a textbook.

Good lecturer, frequent class discussions and group work are interesting and fun. However he is very unclear on his marking scheme. You can get Bs and As on all midterms and presentations based on his scheme and end up with a C- final grade - other components supposidly being worth a combined 20%. States this is standard practice.

John is an okay professor. Just fine. Hes a nice guy and genuinely listens to the class and teaches at a good pace for the learning capabilities of the students. Charisma isnt his strongest attribute but if you work hard your work will pay off. I didnt originally plan on taking this course with him but he was alright.

Class's mostly lecture, with last 30-40 minutes spent practicing what we just learned / doing related exercises. Tests were all m/c, and quizzes are long answers, but neither were particularly hard as long as you've taken a LING course before. Would not recommend him for people who've never taken LING before, since he doesn't simplify things enough
he is a nice person but his lectures are quite boring, he sometimes doesnt know what he is teaching about and cant answer students questions

All over the place, Does not know what he is doing. Often unsure how to analyze the data, but expects us to know how to. Made us do Analytical summaries, but did not teach us prior knowledge.  Basically told us: Life is challenging, so I will give you more challenges. Not helpful during office hours . Leaves right after class. Textbook is better

Terrible professor. Yawns during his lecture and shows no interest in teaching the material as well as shows little care for seeing his students succeed. You ask him for help and he makes it seem like your question and concerns are invalid and pointless. I find this course to be interesting but would recommend with a different professor.

Lectures and lecture slides all over the map, so confusing. He goes WAY too fast, doesn't explain things clearly or thoroughly, and uses terminology that assumes prior knowledge. This is an INTRO course. He also jumps all over the text book instead of following it in a linear fashion to help build on knowledge. Seems like a nice enough guy though.
Terrible teacher , notes are not clear , very confused...what he required book is completely not useful!

Phenomenal professor. Explains everything clearly with many examples. Exam data is given in advance, and multiple choice is right out of the notes/examples covered in class. Fair marker, and rewards critical thinking even if you're not entirely correct. Would take every linguistics course with this man if it were possible. He's brilliant.

John isn't bad at all! He's nice and his lectures are pretty fun if you have a real interest in Linguistics. I'd read the book and not get anything, but when I come to class to attend the lecture, I actually learn stuff.

Not bad at all. The course mainly focuses on 2 aspects, word schema and word syntax. His handwriting appears not good, however, the lec notes he provided were good enough. Textbook reading is recommended.

His basis for marking is unclear. You never know why you got a certain mark on an exam or homework. He doesn't like when you visit his office hours. Sometimes, you will get a 100 on the homework, and other times you will get a 70, but you will never know why.

Giving the final TWO weeks before the semester was over and THEN giving homework is ridiculous. Be prepared to remember specific LANGUAGE examples, even though the class is supposed to be about phonological *PROCESSES*. If I want to learn and remember specific languages, I'd be in a different class.

An extremely frustrating prof. Disorganized & complicated lectures that confuse students. I just read the text and skipped Lec cuz he's so boring and confusing. If I hadn't taken other LING courses before, I'd have been in trouble! Too hard for an Intro course. Makes tutorials a req and assigns homework. Totally killed my interest in LING! AVOID!!!
very good prof.!!

I cant believe people are saying Alderete is a good prof. I was interested in ling before I took this intro class, but now I just feel frustruated and lost because of John. In lec he uses terminology that is way too advanced for an intro class, and when he does pause to ask 'any questions' i dont even know where to begin. The tests are m/c but HARD
he took a subject i hated and thought i was terrible at and made me love it. i did really well, he is a great teacher, albeit a bit nerdy at times.

John is very helpful in the class when you ask some questions. However, he is very serious and mean in his office hour. The exam is very hard. However, if you study his lecture note, at least, you will pass his course. If you have other options, do not take his course. Not very good teacher. Not higly recomended.
He is very emotional. When he is happy, he is good. Overall I think he is not a good prof. The exams are not doable. Essays are very hard.
Good prof.
No Comments

My favourite Linguistics professor at SFU.  He is very thorough AND encourages thinking further than what he's teaching.  His class was a lot of fun.  The final project was the most fun I've had with any project.
can never follow up his lecture with his notes.. but he's helpful in office hour in which he makes sure you totally understand whatever you asked

Very intelligent prof, though very boring & lack of sense of humour(he yawns during his lectures). He tries to avoid ambiguity on exams, but is very picky with your wording on definitions/essays. Exams/assignments focus on his lecture notes which he prepares before lecture.
He's not bad!
No Comments
